title: Microscopic Observational Relativity (MOR): An Ontological Reconstruction of Relative Facts, Unmanifested Domains, and Slice Invariants
description: Microscopic Observational Relativity (MOR) is presented as an ontological interpretational framework for quantum mechanics, not as a replacement dynamics for standard quantum theory. The academic-v0.1 draft positions MOR near relational quantum mechanics, decoherence, quantum Darwinism, and quantum reference frame research, while proposing three discussion points: the ontological status of unmanifested relational structure, the distinction between observational slices and slice-invariant structure, and the possible emergence of macroscopic space from a microscopic relational domain. The weak version does not derive the Born rule, introduce new experimental deviations, or claim to solve the full measurement problem.
notes: This work was independently conceived by a non-professional physics researcher and is published as a public, citable, versioned record. Post-hoc comparison reveals partial overlap with relational quantum mechanics, stable/relative facts, decoherence, quantum Darwinism, quantum reference frame research, and spacetime-from-entanglement literature. Historical versions are preserved in the repository to document the development of the idea. The academic-v0.1 version DOI is 10.5281/zenodo.21068159; the project concept DOI is 10.5281/zenodo.21068158.
